title: phacelia sericea subsp. lyallii
intro: Phacelia sericea subsp. lyallii, also known as New Zealand phacelia, is a subspecies of the Phacelia sericea plant, a member of the borage family (Boraginaceae). This plant has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for wound healing and skin conditions. The health benefits of New Zealand phacelia include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. New Zealand phacelia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
